principle: Update the policy by score-function ascent where each step's gradient is weighted by the per-step empirical copula deviation between the two reward channels' cumulative-return processes (the absolute difference between marginal-empirical-CDF positions of M^1_t and M^2_t).
primitive_type: (time-index t, channel c) -> empirical CDF F-hat_t^c : R -> [0,1] over a rolling buffer of past cumulative-channel values; evaluated on the realized rollout to produce per-step gradient weight d_t = |F-hat_t^1(M^1_t) - F-hat_t^2(M^2_t)| in [0,1]
feedback_signal: per-component vector reward read from info['vector'] each step (m=2 channels: treasure, step penalty); cumulative per-channel values M^c_t are inserted into per-(t, c) ring buffers and used to compute empirical CDF ranks of the current rollout
novelty_boundary: d_t is rank-invariant: any strictly-monotone componentwise transformation of (r^1, r^2) leaves d_t exactly unchanged (Sklar's theorem application), while every linear or non-linear scalarization w^T (phi^1(r^1), phi^2(r^2)) is sensitive to such transformations, so d_t is not equal to any scalarizing functional of the vector reward; d_t is also a per-step weight on a single on-policy rollout rather than a per-trajectory rank (distinguishing it from order-statistics methods like ordergrad/PolicyBoost) or a replay-sampling probability (distinguishing it from rank-based prioritized experience replay), and is computed without any learned value or distributional object
empirical_claim: On the quick stage (deep-sea-treasure-concave-v0, dense step penalty, vector reward), COPDEV produces a strictly larger per-rollout gradnorm_var = Var_t(||g_t||) / Mean_t(||g_t||)^2 than its uniform-weight ablation within 60s of training; this is a logged scalar that is non-zero at random initialization and does not require the policy to find any treasure to fire, so the discriminator is reachable inside the 120s budget independent of whether nearest-treasure trajectories are eventually solved
falsifier: If COPDEV's gradnorm_var equals the uniform-weight ablation's within seed variance during the first 50 episodes (i.e., d_t is empirically constant across t under the substrate's actual rollouts), the copula-deviation primitive is not load-bearing; secondarily, if COPDEV's mean episode length and final hypervolume both match the ablation within seed variance throughout 120s, the mechanism produces no measurable training-dynamics or score-level effect on this substrate
ablation_plan: Replace the per-step weight d_t with the constant 1 (recovering REINFORCE-without-baseline). Concretely in train_ablate.py: skip CDF computation, skip ring-buffer maintenance, set d_t = 1 in the score-function update Sigma_t d_t * grad log pi(a_t|s_t). All other code (rollout, policy net, optimizer, hyperparameters, gradient-norm logging) is preserved. Discriminating logged observables: gradnorm_var (cv-squared of per-step gradient norms within a rollout), mean d_t per rollout, mean episode length, and final hypervolume on DST-concave
proof_debt: (1) Two-timescale Borkar 2008-style convergence of theta_n with the rolling-CDF buffer on the slow timescale, identifying the stationary objective Phi(pi) = E_tau[Sigma_t |F^1_t(M^1_t; pi) - F^2_t(M^2_t; pi)|]. (2) Bias and variance of the rolling-buffer CDF estimator and its propagation to gradient bias. (3) Pareto-improvement claim: do maximizers of Phi lie on or near the Pareto frontier of the achievable vector-return set? (4) Degenerate-channel reduction: when one channel is deterministic (DST-concave's step penalty), characterize when the survival-rank variant remains structurally distinct from existing methods.
